description_long: This object is a streamlined, cylindrical airplane with a pointed nose and a smooth, continuous fuselage. The rear features four prominent engine nozzles arranged symmetrically around the central axis, suggesting a jet propulsion system. The wings are not visible, indicating they may be integrated into the lower fuselage or retracted, and the tail section is compact with subtle control surfaces. The surface is uniformly smooth and painted in a light, glossy color, with minimal visible markings or windows. There are no visible landing gear components, and the overall design is reminiscent of a modern, possibly unmanned, high-speed aircraft. The object appears to be constructed primarily from metal, likely aluminum or a composite material, with some internal components possibly made of other materials.
description: A sleek, cylindrical airplane with a pointed nose, four rear jet nozzles, and a smooth, glossy exterior. The wings are not visible, and the surface is uniformly light-colored.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from the nose, showing its pointed tip and symmetrical, streamlined body.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the elongated fuselage, smooth surface, and absence of visible wings or landing gear.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights four large jet nozzles and a compact tail section with subtle control surfaces.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile mirrors view 1, showing the same streamlined fuselage and lack of external features.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with wings integrated into the lower fuselage, no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles, straight design.
attribute_engine_type: Quad jet engines, rear-mounted in a clustered arrangement.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a streamlined canopy, likely single-seat.
